title: Tunisian Squid Pasta
description: A salty, aerated, and festive Tunisian dish featuring squid, pasta, and a water emulsion, suitable for a Jain-friendly diet. This recipe combines the flavors of the Mediterranean with the simplicity of a weeknight meal.

ingredients.name: squid
ingredients.notes: cleaned and cut into bite-sized pieces

instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the pasta according to the package instructions until al dente.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat and add the garlic, cooking for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the squid to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes until opaque and slightly browned.
Add the lemon juice, water, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer the squid mixture for 5 minutes until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Stir in the chopped parsley and harissa, if using.
Drain the cooked pasta and add it to the skillet, tossing to combine with the squid sauce.
Serve the Tunisian squid pasta hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
